name: Paradoxornithidae
description: Parrotbills are a family of small to medium-sized passerine birds found primarily in East and Southeast Asia. They are named for their distinctive, parrot-like bills adapted for splitting bamboo stems and seed pods.

children.name: Suthora
children.description: A genus of small parrotbills with short, deep bills, found in bamboo thickets across Asia.

children.name: Suthora webbiana
children.description: The Vinous-throated Parrotbill is a small, brownish bird with a vinous-pink throat and a short, stout bill. It is common in bamboo and reed beds across East Asia, often moving in noisy flocks.

children.name: Suthora alphonsiana
children.description: The Ashy-throated Parrotbill has warm brown upperparts, a grey throat and breast, and a small parrot-like bill. It inhabits bamboo and scrub in montane regions of southern China and mainland Southeast Asia.

children.name: Suthora nipalensis
children.description: A parrotbill from the Himalayas and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Suthora ruficeps
children.description: A parrotbill with rufous head from the Himalayas.

children.name: Suthora fulvifrons
children.description: A parrotbill with fulvous forehead from the Himalayas and western China.

children.name: Suthora verreauxi
children.description: A golden parrotbill from the Himalayas and western China.

children.name: Suthora princeps
children.description: A brown parrotbill from the Himalayas.

children.name: Suthora brunnea
children.description: A parrotbill with brown wings from the Himalayas.

children.name: Suthora zapponi
children.description: A parrotbill from southern China and Vietnam.

children.name: Suthora muriela
children.description: A parrotbill with black ear patch from western China.

children.name: Paradoxornis
children.description: A genus of larger parrotbills with deep, robust bills, found in reed beds and bamboo across Asia.

children.name: Paradoxornis heudei
children.description: The Reed Parrotbill is a large parrotbill with a stout orange bill and bold black facial stripes. It inhabits dense reed beds along rivers and lakes in eastern China.

children.name: Paradoxornis aemodium
children.description: The Great Parrotbill is the largest of the parrotbills, with a massive, deep bill and striking black-and-white head pattern. It inhabits high-altitude bamboo thickets in the Himalayas.

children.name: Paradoxornis albirostris
children.description: A parrotbill with white bill from the Himalayas.

children.name: Paradoxornis poliotis
children.description: A parrotbill with grey head from southwestern China.

children.name: Paradoxornis marginatus
children.description: A parrotbill with spotted breast from southeastern Asia.

children.name: Paradoxornis gularis
children.description: A parrotbill with grey hood from the Himalayas to Southeast Asia.

children.name: Paradoxornis flaviventer
children.description: A parrotbill with yellow belly from the eastern Himalayas.

children.name: Paradoxornis guttaticollis
children.description: A parrotbill with spotted throat from southeastern Asia.

children.name: Paradoxornis concolor
children.description: A rufous-headed parrotbill from the eastern Himalayas.

children.name: Paradoxornis unicolor
children.description: A brown parrotbill from the Himalayas.

children.name: Paradoxornis ruficeps
children.description: A large rufous-headed parrotbill from the eastern Himalayas.

children.name: Paradoxornis striatus
children.description: A striated parrotbill from central China.

children.name: Paradoxornis davidianus
children.description: A parrotbill from southeastern China and northern Vietnam.

children.name: Paradoxornis atrosuperciliaris
children.description: A parrotbill with black eyebrow from the eastern Himalayas and western China.

children.name: Paradoxornis conspicillatus
children.description: A parrotbill with eye-ring from central China.

children.name: Paradoxornis przewalskii
children.description: A parrotbill from western China.

children.name: Paradoxornis nigriventris
children.description: A parrotbill with black throat from the Himalayas and western China.

children.name: Paradoxornis brunneus
children.description: A brown parrotbill from southern China and Myanmar.

children.name: Paradoxornis zapponi
children.description: A parrotbill from southern China and Vietnam.

children.name: Paradoxornis ricketti
children.description: A parrotbill from southern China and Vietnam.
